title: Early Childhood Development Measurement And Intervention Coverage Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping early childhood development outcomes and intervention-coverage gaps at sub-national scale in LMICs, with explicit MICS ECDI instrument limitations, preschool-quality variation documentation, and the distinction between intervention coverage and verified developmental impact.
why_it_matters: WHO and UNICEF estimate 250 million children under five in LMICs are at risk of not reaching their developmental potential. Preschool enrollment in low-income countries averages below 20 percent with enormous sub-national variation. Parenting interventions show 0.3-0.5 standard-deviation improvements in trials but coverage is below 5 percent. Without verified sub-national ECD-outcome and coverage analysis, early childhood investment cannot be evidence-targeted where it would most improve developmental outcomes.

success_metrics.target: Every ECD-outcome claim documents MICS ECDI instrument comparability across countries and survey rounds.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against ECD-measurement methodology documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every coverage claim documents sub-national variation and explicitly states where district-level data is absent.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms sub-national granularity and coverage-gap documentation.

success_metrics.target: No developmental-impact claim is merged without documenting that intervention coverage does not equal verified developmental impact without quality and dosage data.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms coverage-vs-impact distinction in every effectiveness claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, ECD-measurement safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
